name: Maldivian (Dhivehi)
notes: Grapheme inventory NOT YET SPECIFIED (stub). Dhivehi is written in Thaana, right-to-left. Thaana is precisely an alphabet-like abugida: its consonant letters carry NO inherent vowel, and each is obligatorily accompanied by a fili — one of the ten vowel diacritics, or the sukun marking absence of a vowel — so every vowel IS written (unlike a true abjad), yet vowels remain diacritics on a consonant base rather than independent letters (unlike a true alphabet). `abugida` is the closest of the schema's categories; because Thaana has no default vowel, no `inherent_vowel` is declared. The older Dhives Akuru script and the Malé Latin transliteration are not modelled here. No Thaana-to-IPA map is claimed. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode dhiv1236), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.
